title: Grastidium stictanthum
taxonomy: Grastidium stictanthum is classified within the family Restionaceae, commonly known as the restio family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the southern hemisphere, particularly in Australia and New Zealand. The genus Grastidium itself is part of the tribe Chondrachneae. As a member of the restio family, Grastidium stictanthum is closely related to other species such as Leptocarpus and Chondrachne. Further classification places Grastidium stictanthum as part of the order Poales.
